itinerary.mapNote: Descend dramatically into the Apurímac canyon. Tropical heat. 1550m.
Cross the Apurímac River suspension bridge. Remote canyon camp. 1480m.
Long steep climb to the ruins. The citadel emerges from the cloud forest. 3085m.
Full morning exploring 'the other Machu Picchu'. Llama terraces. 2850m.
Long return ascent out of the canyon. Stunning views all the way. 2900m.

waypoints.name: Choquequirao Citadel
waypoints.note: Major Inca royal estate. Only 30% excavated. Far fewer crowds than Machu Picchu.

waypoints.name: Apurímac Canyon
waypoints.note: One of the world's deepest canyons. The Inca called it 'God of speech'.

waypoints.name: Llama Terraces
waypoints.note: Unique white llama inlaid in the terracing — only found at Choquequirao.

waypoints.name: Capuliyoq Pass
waypoints.note: Gateway views into the canyon. Cachora village visible below.
